comment: The revised business grant scheme genuinely helped us survive the slow season.
Another property tax hike with zero improvement in local services is inexcusable.
Corporate incentives for the new tech park will create high-paying jobs. Strong policy.
Hiking sales tax on essentials during a cost-of-living crisis shows total disregard for families.
The economic regeneration zone has brought three new employers to our district this year.
The council's budget transparency portal is excellent — every citizen should use it.
Hidden fees in the new business licensing process are driving small traders away.
Reducing stamp duty for first-time buyers is exactly the kind of targeted relief we needed.
The segregated cycle highway on the waterfront is world-class — our city finally gets it.
Unrepaired sinkholes on the industrial estate have damaged two delivery vehicles this month.
The new intercity rail link will transform commuting and reduce congestion enormously.
Bus routes cut by 40% since last year — rural communities are completely isolated now.
EV charging stations in every council car park by year-end is an ambitious but achievable target.
The harbour bridge closure with zero alternative route was catastrophically mismanaged.
Pavement tactile paving upgrades across the city centre have improved accessibility significantly.
Flood drainage on the south ring road is completely inadequate — it floods every winter.
Smart traffic signals at the five busiest junctions have cut rush-hour delays by 18%.
The long-promised footbridge connecting the station to the business park is still not built.
The community land trust pilot in Westgate is the most progressive housing policy in years.
Retrospective planning approval for the illegal riverside development sets a terrible precedent.
Purpose-built student accommodation near the university is relieving pressure on family housing.
Permitted development rights abuse is converting quality office space into substandard flats.
The rent-to-buy scheme gave my family a realistic pathway to ownership after seven years of renting.
Green-belt encroachment for luxury estates while social housing waiting lists grow is unforgivable.
Retrofit grants for Victorian terraces will reduce emissions and fuel poverty simultaneously.
The planning portal redesign is a genuine improvement — applications take half the time now.
Mixed-tenure social housing developments foster much healthier communities than segregated estates.
The river rewilding project has restored otter and kingfisher populations. Remarkable work.
Industrial effluent discharge near the estuary has not been properly investigated despite reports.
Community allotment expansion programme has a two-year waiting list — scale it up urgently.
The new air quality monitoring network finally gives us real-time data to hold polluters accountable.
Replacing grass verges with wildflower meadows is beautiful cheap and brilliant for biodiversity.
Single-use plastic ban in council-run venues should extend to all licensed premises immediately.
The waste-to-energy plant proposal near primary schools is deeply alarming and must be refused.
Urban tree canopy target of 25% by 2030 is ambitious and the right direction for climate resilience.
Contaminated land remediation at the old gasworks site is long overdue — residents deserve answers.
Neighbourhood watch digital integration with the council portal has visibly reduced burglaries.
Knife crime intervention programmes for at-risk youth deserve far more sustained investment.
Ambulance response times in rural postcodes remain dangerously above the national target.
The new community safety hub brings police social services and housing under one roof. Excellent.
CCTV expansion without a clear data governance policy is surveillance creep not public safety.
The fire station closure in Millbrook leaves 15000 residents with unacceptably slow coverage.
Early years literacy programme results show a 22% improvement in school readiness. Keep investing.
Adult education centre cuts mean workers cannot access the retraining they need after redundancy.
Free school meals extension to all primary pupils is a straightforward public health intervention.
The new community hub model co-locating library health and job-centre services is inspired.
Special educational needs funding shortfall is forcing schools to exclude children who need support.
Digital inclusion tablets for care home residents bridging the connectivity gap — wonderful scheme.
Closing the only youth centre in Northfield will push teenagers onto streets. Short-sighted saving.
Graduate retention grants for healthcare professionals have reduced vacancy rates by a third.
